accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context about Section 546 of the PHSA first responder training program.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the PHSA program identification is accurate context confirmed by sources.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill reauthorizes and updates the first responder training program.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies that the bill amends Section 546 of the Public Health Service Act and describes the specific amendments.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions, conditions, or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: Notes the authorization period of fiscal years 2026 through 2030.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all operative effects: scope expansion beyond opioids, terminology updates, and funding reauthorization.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and free of editorializing or advocacy.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately concise without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Public Health Service Act, Section 546, and the first responder training program.

correct_figures.why: The figures ($36 million to $57 million) and fiscal years (2019–2023 to 2026–2030) all match the bill text.
